01_MH-0312_narrative.md.reviewed_text:
DOC_ID: MH-0312-NARR
CASE: MH-2023-0312
JURISDICTION: Maple Heights Police Department (Clearwater County)
TYPE: Residential burglary — incident narrative
DATE OF OFFENSE: 2023-03-03
REPORTING DETECTIVE: A. Cole, Badge 4471

NARRATIVE:
At approximately 02:40 hours, a residence at 14 Maple Heights Drive was entered without
consent. Point of entry was the rear sliding glass door, which was pried from its track.
The home office was targeted: a laptop, an external drive, and a small floor safe (~30 lb)
were removed. Entry-to-exit time is estimated at under 15 minutes based on neighbor audio.

The residence has a doorbell camera; at the time of the offense its lens was found
covered with an opaque film/spray, consistent with deliberate obstruction prior to entry.
No usable footage was recovered. No fingerprints recovered (gloves suspected).

Homeowner was away. Loss estimated at $4,200. No suspect identified at time of report.
Case status: OPEN. See forensic supplement MH-0312-FOR and witness statement MH-0312-WIT.

02_MH-0312_forensics.md.reviewed_text:
DOC_ID: MH-0312-FOR
CASE: MH-2023-0312
JURISDICTION: Maple Heights Police Department (Clearwater County)
TYPE: Forensic / tool-mark supplement
DATE: 2023-03-05
EXAMINER: J. Pratt, Forensic Unit

TOOL-MARK ANALYSIS:
Pry marks on the rear sliding door frame are consistent with a flat-head pry bar.
Blade width measured at 8 mm (±0.3 mm). A repeatable defect — a small nick/chip on the
LEFT edge of the blade — is impressed in three of the four mark sets, suggesting a single
tool with a distinctive signature. This signature is suitable for comparison should a
candidate tool be recovered.

TRACE: No latent prints. Glove fabric impression (smooth nitrile) on door handle.

INVESTIGATIVE NOTE: MO (rear-slider pry entry, camera obstruction, electronics + small
safe, overnight window) resembles patterns seen outside this jurisdiction.
RECOMMENDATION: cross-reference a REGIONAL / multi-jurisdiction burglary database for the
8 mm left-nick tool signature.
STATUS OF RECOMMENDATION: NOT ACTIONED — no regional database access available to this
department at time of filing.

03_MH-0312_witness.md.reviewed_text:
DOC_ID: MH-0312-WIT
CASE: MH-2023-0312
JURISDICTION: Maple Heights Police Department (Clearwater County)
TYPE: Witness statement
DATE: 2023-03-03
WITNESS: Neighbor, 18 Maple Heights Drive (insomnia, walking dog)

STATEMENT (summarized):
"Around 2:30 in the morning I saw a dark blue four-door sedan parked across from number
14 with its lights off. It wasn't a car I recognized from the street. When I came back
maybe ten minutes later it was pulling away, no headlights until the corner. I only caught
part of the plate — it started with 8 K, and I think there was a number after. Driver was
alone, hood up. Didn't think much of it until I heard about the break-in."

OFFICER NOTE: partial plate "8K?" logged; no DMV hit on partial alone.

04_RV-0788_narrative.md.reviewed_text:
DOC_ID: RV-0788-NARR
CASE: RV-2023-0788
JURISDICTION: Riverside Police Department (Hale County)
TYPE: Residential burglary — incident narrative
DATE OF OFFENSE: 2023-11-19
REPORTING DETECTIVE: M. Okafor, Badge 2210

NARRATIVE:
Between approximately 03:00 and 03:30 hours, a residence at 902 Riverside Lane was
burglarized. Entry was through the rear sliding glass door, forced from its track. The
home office and a bedroom closet were searched; a laptop, two external drives, and a
lockbox were taken. The household's smart doorbell recorded only a dark frame — the lens
appears to have been sprayed or covered immediately before entry.

No prints recovered; gloves suspected. Occupants asleep upstairs, heard nothing. Estimated
loss $3,600. No suspect identified.
Case status: OPEN. Forensic supplement: RV-0788-FOR. Witness statement: RV-0788-WIT.

NOTE: Riverside PD records are maintained in the Hale County system, which does not share
indexing with neighboring Clearwater County.

05_RV-0788_forensics.md.reviewed_text:
DOC_ID: RV-0788-FOR
CASE: RV-2023-0788
JURISDICTION: Riverside Police Department (Hale County)
TYPE: Forensic / tool-mark supplement
DATE: 2023-11-21
EXAMINER: L. Hartman, Regional Forensic Services

TOOL-MARK ANALYSIS:
Pry damage to the sliding-door frame indicates a flat-bladed pry tool approximately 8 mm
in width. A consistent casting defect — a chip on ONE edge of the blade (left, relative to
the impressions) — recurs across the mark sets. The signature is distinct enough to match
a recovered tool if one is found. Photographs and silicone casts retained.

TRACE: Smooth-glove transfer on the interior handle; no ridge detail.

INVESTIGATIVE NOTE: entry method and tooling are characteristic of a serial offender. A
REGIONAL DATABASE CHECK is recommended to compare the 8 mm single-edge-nick signature
against burglaries in adjacent counties.
STATUS OF RECOMMENDATION: PENDING — request submitted, no regional system available;
follow-up not completed.

06_RV-0788_witness.md.reviewed_text:
DOC_ID: RV-0788-WIT
CASE: RV-2023-0788
JURISDICTION: Riverside Police Department (Hale County)
TYPE: Witness statement
DATE: 2023-11-19
WITNESS: Rideshare driver, dropping a fare two doors down

STATEMENT (summarized):
"I dropped someone off around 3-ish on Riverside Lane. There was a dark blue sedan, older
model, sitting with no lights near the end of the block. Looked empty but the engine was
running — I noticed the exhaust. When I pulled away a guy in a hoodie was walking toward
it carrying a bag. Didn't get the plate, sorry. Just the color and that it was a plain
four-door, dark blue."

OFFICER NOTE: vehicle description (dark blue four-door sedan, engine running, overnight)
logged. No plate captured.

07_MH-0102_narrative.md.reviewed_text:
DOC_ID: MH-0102-NARR
CASE: MH-2025-0102
JURISDICTION: Maple Heights Police Department (Clearwater County)
TYPE: Residential burglary — incident narrative
DATE OF OFFENSE: 2025-02-04
REPORTING DETECTIVE: A. Cole, Badge 4471

NARRATIVE:
At approximately 03:10 hours, 31 Maple Heights Court was entered via the rear sliding
glass door, pried from its track. Home office targeted: laptop, camera gear, and a small
safe removed. Estimated loss $5,100. Gloves suspected; no prints.

DIFFERENCE FROM PRIOR PATTERN: the offender attempted to obscure the doorbell camera but
the spray missed the lens edge. A partial clip (4 seconds) captured a dark blue sedan at
the curb and a PARTIAL PLATE. See arrest report MH-0102-ARR.

DETECTIVE NOTE (Cole): "This is the third job on my desk in two years with the exact same
signature — rear slider, overnight, camera covered, electronics + a small safe. I've
believed for a while it's one person. I never had anything to connect them to another
jurisdiction." Forensic supplement: MH-0102-FOR.

08_MH-0102_forensics.md.reviewed_text:
DOC_ID: MH-0102-FOR
CASE: MH-2025-0102
JURISDICTION: Maple Heights Police Department (Clearwater County)
TYPE: Forensic / tool-mark supplement
DATE: 2025-02-06
EXAMINER: J. Pratt, Forensic Unit

TOOL-MARK ANALYSIS:
Pry marks on the rear door frame match a flat blade of ~8 mm width bearing a nick on the
left edge — consistent with the signature documented in this department's 2023 case
MH-2023-0312. Casts retained for comparison against any recovered tool.

EXAMINER NOTE: "Same tool signature as the 2023 Maple Heights job, in my opinion. If a
suspect tool is recovered, direct comparison should be conclusive."

09_MH-0102_arrest.md.reviewed_text:
DOC_ID: MH-0102-ARR
CASE: MH-2025-0102
JURISDICTION: Maple Heights Police Department (Clearwater County)
TYPE: Arrest / disposition report
DATE: 2025-02-10
REPORTING DETECTIVE: A. Cole, Badge 4471

SUMMARY:
The 4-second doorbell clip from 31 Maple Heights Court captured a dark blue sedan and a
partial plate beginning "8K". A DMV query on the partial plus vehicle make returned a
registered owner: DANIEL R. MARSH, 34. A search warrant on Marsh's vehicle and garage
recovered a flat-head pry bar with an 8 mm blade and a chip on the left edge.

FORENSIC COMPARISON: the recovered pry bar matched the tool-mark casts from MH-2025-0102,
MH-2023-0312 (Maple Heights), AND — after a forensic examiner flagged the similarity to a
colleague — the casts from RV-2023-0788 (Riverside, Hale County). Marsh subsequently
admitted to all three burglaries.

DISPOSITION NOTE (Cole): "The Riverside connection was never in any system I could search.
We only found it because two examiners happened to talk. The plate was luck. ~23 months
and three families, and the link was sitting in two filing cabinets the whole time."

10_MARSH_alibi_statement.md.reviewed_text:
DOC_ID: MARSH-ALIBI
CASE: MH-2025-0102 (cross-references RV-2023-0788)
JURISDICTION: Maple Heights Police Department (Clearwater County)
TYPE: Suspect statement — alibi
DATE: 2025-02-10
RECORDING DETECTIVE: A. Cole, Badge 4471

STATEMENT (Daniel R. Marsh, summarized):
When asked about the Riverside burglary on the night of 2023-11-19, Marsh stated:
"I was nowhere near Riverside. That whole week in November 2023 I was out of state —
visiting family in Pineford, about 300 miles away. I didn't drive back until later that
month. You can't put me at Riverside on the 19th; I wasn't even in the county."

Marsh declined to provide names of family members present. No corroborating travel
records were offered by the suspect.

11_MARSH_financial_records.md.reviewed_text:
DOC_ID: MARSH-RECEIPT
CASE: RV-2023-0788 (obtained during MH-2025-0102 investigation)
JURISDICTION: Riverside Police Department (Hale County) — records subpoena
TYPE: Financial / lodging records
DATE OF RECORDS: 2023-11-18 to 2023-11-19
ANALYST: Financial Crimes liaison

RECORDS SUMMARY (debit card ending 4417, registered to Daniel R. Marsh):
- 2023-11-18, 23:31 — fuel purchase, Hale County Fuel Stop, 6.1 miles from 902 Riverside Lane.
- 2023-11-19, 00:48 — lodging check-in, Hale County Motor Lodge, 4.2 miles from 902
  Riverside Lane. One night, paid by the same card. Front-desk record lists a dark blue
  sedan in the registration notes.

INVESTIGATIVE NOTE: These records place Marsh within ~5 miles of the Riverside burglary
scene in the hours around the 2023-11-19 offense — directly contradicting his statement
(MARSH-ALIBI) that he was 300 miles out of state that night.
